reasoning: The biconditional in wf correctly constrains set elements to [0, N), so the set cardinality is bounded by N. This is an expected consequence of a well-designed invariant.
This is a standard property of `Seq::update` from vstd — updating index i preserves index j when i != j. Not dependent on external_body.

The wf biconditional ensures `set.contains(i) ==> data[i]` and `data[i] ==> set.contains(i)`. With an empty set, no element is contained, so the reverse direction forces all data entries to false. Correct and expected.
Both wf() instances enforce `self.len == self.set@.len()`, and view gives `self.set@`. Equal views mean equal set lengths, so equal len fields. Expected consequence of the invariant.
